name: 颜金勇
current_positions: Chairman of the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS)
Chairman of EDB International Advisory Council
MP for Punggol GRC (Punggol North ward)

work_experience.position: Various roles, rising from Executive to President and CEO (2005)
Minister of State for Education; Minister of State for Manpower

work_experience.organization: Parliament of Singapore (Chua Chu Kang SMC)

work_experience.position: Acting Minister (2008-2009), then Minister for Manpower (2009-2011)

work_experience.organization: Parliament of Singapore (Chua Chu Kang GRC)

work_experience.position: Deputy Prime Minister (sole DPM; Acting PM in PM Wong's absence)

person_relationships.description: Co-chaired COVID-19 multi-ministerial committee; serves as sole DPM under PM Wong
Long-time cabinet colleague under PM Lee; served as PAP Chairman (2018-2022)
Cabinet colleague; both serve in Lawrence Wong's cabinet
Preceded Gan as Minister for Health and as PAP Chairman
Former DPM alongside Gan (2019-2025); Gan succeeded Heng as sole DPM

political_stances.stance_content: As Health Minister, launched the 'War on Diabetes' initiative to combat rising diabetes rates in Singapore

Co-chaired the Multi-Ministry Taskforce on COVID-19 with Lawrence Wong; managed Singapore's pandemic response including circuit breaker measures, foreign worker dormitory crisis, and vaccination rollout
As Trade and Industry Minister, oversees Singapore's economic strategies including trade agreements, industry transformation, and economic resilience
Questioned in open letter by former Income Insurance CEO Tan Suee Chieh about MAS's lapse of regulatory oversight and his role as former chairman of Singapore Labour Foundation
During GE2025, claimed he was 'good friends' with PM Wong and would secure more infrastructure funds for Punggol; Workers' Party criticised this as undermining civil service impartiality

major_achievements.achievement: Rose to President and CEO of NatSteel before entering full-time politics
Appointed Acting Minister for Manpower, managing Singapore's labor policy during the global financial crisis
Appointed Minister for Health; served for 10 years, the longest-serving Health Minister
Led PAP team to win Chua Chu Kang GRC with 76.89% of vote (highest margin in GE2015)
Launched the 'War on Diabetes' national initiative
Appointed by the World Health Organization to serve on the Commission on Non-Communicable Diseases
Co-chaired multi-ministerial COVID-19 taskforce with Lawrence Wong, managing the entire pandemic response
Appointed sole Deputy Prime Minister and Acting PM in PM Wong's absence; also Chairman of MAS and EDB IAC
Appointed Director of GIC, Singapore's sovereign wealth fund
Led PAP team to win newly-formed Punggol GRC with 55.17% against Workers' Party team led by Harpreet Singh Nehal

biography_summary: Gan Kim Yong (born 9 February 1959) is a Singaporean politician serving as sole Deputy Prime Minister since 2024 and Minister for Trade and Industry since 2021. He concurrently chairs the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS), chairs the EDB International Advisory Council, and serves as a GIC Director. Educated at Catholic High School, National Junior College, and the University of Cambridge (BA and MA in Engineering) under an Overseas Merit Scholarship, he began his career in the civil service before joining NatSteel in 1989, rising to President and CEO by 2005. He entered politics in 2001, representing Holland-Bukit Panjang GRC, then Chua Chu Kang SMC/GRC, and now Punggol GRC. He served as Minister for Manpower (2008-2011) during the global financial crisis, Minister for Health (2011-2021) for a record 10 years overseeing the War on Diabetes and COVID-19 response, Chairman of PAP (2018-2022), and now sole DPM under PM Lawrence Wong. He was appointed to the WHO Commission on Non-Communicable Diseases in 2018. He is married to Esther Loh with two daughters, and is a Presbyterian elder at Chen Li Presbyterian Church.

collection_meta.notes: Comprehensive enrichment with additional political positions (PAP Whip, SLF Chairman, MAS Deputy Chairman, EDB IAC Chairman, GIC Director), WHO appointment, War on Diabetes initiative, COVID-19 taskforce details, GE2025 Punggol GRC contest. Added predecessor relationships (Khaw Boon Wan, Heng Swee Keat). Birth date uses Wikipedia (1959-02-09); Wikidata had conflicting date (1952-12-08).
